quickStart.title: Get productive in 30 seconds
steps: Open the AI side panel with Cmd/Ctrl+I (or Cmd/Ctrl+L).
Start in Agent mode for normal coding tasks; press Shift+Tab to rotate modes if you want Ask, Plan, or Debug.
For tiny scoped edits, select code and hit Cmd/Ctrl+K for Inline Edit instead of opening a full agent run.
Use Cmd/Ctrl+/ to cycle models if the default feels too slow, too expensive, or too weak for the task.
When a task is complex, switch to Plan mode first, review the virtual plan file, then click Build.
If work should keep running away from your machine, move the task to Cloud Agents after committing or stashing local changes.

dailyWorkflows.title: Feature build in Agent mode
dailyWorkflows.description: Use Agent as the default multi-file worker: it explores the repo, edits files, runs commands, and self-corrects errors.
dailyWorkflows.command: Cmd/Ctrl+I → describe the feature clearly → review diffs/checkpoints as it works

dailyWorkflows.title: Plan before touching code
dailyWorkflows.description: For risky or ambiguous work, generate a plan first, inspect it as a virtual file, then approve implementation.
dailyWorkflows.command: Cmd/Ctrl+I → Shift+Tab until Plan → prompt → review plan → Build

dailyWorkflows.title: Inline edit for surgical changes
dailyWorkflows.description: Stay in the editor for focused rewrites, refactors, and transforms without opening a full chat flow.
dailyWorkflows.command: Select code → Cmd/Ctrl+K → type change request → Return

dailyWorkflows.title: Ask question about selected code
dailyWorkflows.description: Use inline question mode when you need explanation before editing.
dailyWorkflows.command: Select code → Cmd/Ctrl+K → Opt/Alt+Return → ask question

dailyWorkflows.title: Use Tab as navigation + fixup engine
dailyWorkflows.description: Accept multi-line suggestions, then hit Tab again to jump to the next predicted edit location, even across files.
dailyWorkflows.command: Type normally → Tab to accept suggestion → Tab again for jump-in-file/cross-file follow-up

dailyWorkflows.title: Push long-running work to Cloud Agents
dailyWorkflows.description: Offload feature work, testing, PR creation, and browser validation to isolated cloud VMs.
dailyWorkflows.command: Choose Cloud under agent input or use cursor.com/agents after committing/stashing local edits

powerMoves.title: Queue the next prompt while Agent is still busy
powerMoves.description: Submit follow-up instructions before the current run ends; reorder queued items if priorities change.
powerMoves.command: Type next instruction during an active run and let it queue behind the current task
powerMoves.why: Keeps momentum high and reduces idle time between agent runs.

powerMoves.title: Promote a hard local task into a cloud run
powerMoves.description: Use Cloud Agents when the work involves test runs, browser verification, or should continue unattended.
powerMoves.command: Move to Cloud / pick Cloud target after syncing repo state
powerMoves.why: Cloud Agents can run in isolated VMs, attach screenshots/videos/logs, and even open PRs.

powerMoves.title: Codify recurring instructions as project rules
powerMoves.description: Put durable guidance in .cursor/rules instead of repeating it in prompts every day.
powerMoves.command: Create .md/.mdc files in .cursor/rules or use /create-rule from chat
powerMoves.why: Rules apply across Agent, Ask, Plan, and Debug, giving consistent behavior with less prompt overhead.

powerMoves.title: Use subagents for parallel research
powerMoves.description: Let Agent fan out specialized work like research, browser tasks, or shell-heavy subtasks.
powerMoves.command: Ask Agent to research/inspect in parallel, or define custom subagents in .cursor/agents/
powerMoves.why: Parallel sub-contexts reduce main-thread overload and speed up broad investigations.

gotchas.problem: Move to Cloud does not include dirty local files or uncommitted edits.
gotchas.solution: Commit or stash before moving a conversation to Cloud Agents, because cloud runs start from a fresh remote clone.

gotchas.problem: Switching between Ask/Agent/Plan/Debug starts a fresh context window.
gotchas.solution: Treat mode switches like new contexts; restate critical constraints or start a new chat cleanly when the task changes.

gotchas.problem: Old advice around .cursorrules can be stale.
gotchas.solution: Prefer modern project rules in .cursor/rules (or AGENTS.md for simpler instructions); reserve legacy .cursorrules knowledge for interoperability only.

gotchas.problem: Plans are saved to your home directory by default, not automatically into the repo.
gotchas.solution: Hit Save to workspace if the plan should live with the project or be shared with teammates.

gotchas.problem: Cloud Agents require eligible plans/on-demand usage and only expose Max Mode-compatible models.
gotchas.solution: Verify billing/on-demand usage and model availability before depending on cloud runs in a workflow.

slashCommands.description: Generate a project rule file in .cursor/rules from a natural-language description.
slashCommands.example: /create-rule Add a frontend rule that enforces Tailwind and points to our canonical button component.
slashCommands.tip: Use it after you catch yourself repeating the same prompt instruction more than twice.

slashCommands.description: Launch the main AI panel for Agent/Ask/Plan/Debug workflows.
slashCommands.tip: This is the fastest entry point for all multi-file work.

slashCommands.description: Edit selected code in place without a full chat session.
slashCommands.example: Select code → Cmd/Ctrl+K → 'extract this into a helper'
slashCommands.tip: Great for transforms that fit inside one file or one selection.

slashCommands.description: Switch between Agent, Ask, Plan, and Debug modes.
slashCommands.example: Cmd/Ctrl+. for mode menu, Shift+Tab to rotate modes
slashCommands.tip: Default to Agent; switch to Plan only when the approach itself needs review.

slashCommands.description: Rotate between available models from the keyboard.
slashCommands.tip: Useful when one model is overkill for routine edits or underpowered for deep refactors.

config.description: Version-controlled markdown or .mdc rule files that steer Agent behavior per repo, path, or manual invocation.
config.default: Off until you create files in .cursor/rules
config.recommended: Create small focused rules with descriptions/globs instead of one giant prompt dump.

config.description: Simple markdown alternative to rules for straightforward agent instructions at the repo root or subdirectories.
config.recommended: Use when you want human-readable guidance without frontmatter complexity.

config.description: Controls AI tab completion globally, per extension, or with temporary snooze windows.
config.recommended: Disable for noisy filetypes like Markdown/JSON if suggestions become distracting.

config.description: Choose Auto, Premium, Composer, or a specific frontier model in the picker; selection persists across conversations.
config.recommended: Use Auto or Composer for everyday flow; switch to premium frontier models for high-stakes multi-step work.

config.description: Repository connection, secrets/environment variables, and optional MCP servers for cloud runs.
config.recommended: Complete onboarding early for repos you touch often so offloading work is a one-click option.

proTips: Use Agent as the default, Inline Edit for surgical changes, and Plan only when the approach needs approval before code lands.
After accepting a Tab suggestion, press Tab again to follow Cursor's predicted next edit location instead of manually hunting through files.
Keep rules under roughly 500 lines and point to canonical files rather than pasting large style guides into the prompt context.
If you want a reusable plan artifact, save the plan into the workspace immediately; otherwise it lives outside the repo by default.
Cloud Agents are strongest when the repo is clean, secrets are preconfigured, and the task benefits from browser testing or PR automation.
